_provenance.variantRole: Surface fork of B — Maximal cinema (loud register)
_provenance.variantThesis: Adobe's full motion bench, on every primary surface. Same captured trait as B (scroll choreography), intensified across all 6 surface axes.
_provenance.non-canonical-pattern: B3 is a surface fork of B (motion+density+scale tunings of B's scroll-cinema role), not a distinct captured-trait amplification. Officially surface forks are A1/A2/A3 under verbatim ia-fidelity, and we're reimagined. Documented here as parent-direction-tuning, not a new role.

scaleAudit.rationale: B3 amplifies type-scale energy. Perfect-fifth (1.5) is the loudest canonical scale; gives B3 a recognisable louder rhythm vs B's A-inherited clamp ratio ~1.43.

title-4._b3-note: bumped from A's 24px to 32px to maintain perfect-fifth proportion vs t3

_b3-overrides._note: title-1 max 108px at extreme viewport (vs B/A's 80px). title-4 bumped to 32px to keep ratio. body H3s at 700 (vs A's 400) for heavier register.
packed tier — multi-audience hard floor is ≤64px desktop, ≥40px floor. 48px sits inside the band. compact rhythm.

spacing._hard-floor-check: Multi-audience trigger fires (>2 audience tracks per IA-priority audit). Hard floor: sectionPadding.desktop ∈ [40, 64]. B3's 48px clears.

components.name: parallax-host
components.css: .ds-parallax-host { position: relative; overflow: hidden; will-change: transform; } /* JS: rAF computes progress = clamp((vh - rect.top) / (vh + rect.height), 0, 1); childEl.style.transform = translateY((progress - 0.5) * rect.height * 0.4) */
components.reservation: Featured-products section + similar full-bleed photographic media on B3 only

components.name: scroll-scrub-host
components.css: .ds-scroll-scrub-host { } .ds-scroll-scrub-item { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(24px); will-change: opacity, transform; } /* JS: for each item i, p = clamp((progress - stages[i]) / 0.15, 0, 1); el.style.opacity = p; el.style.transform = translateY((1-p) * 24px) */
components.reservation: news section's 3-up grid on B3; pattern extensible to any N-item grid where staged reveal makes sense

newChoreographies.math: rate = 0.6; progress = clamp((vh - rect.top) / (vh + rect.height), 0, 1); mediaEl.transform = translateY((progress - 0.5) * rect.height * (1 - rate))
stages = [0.2, 0.5, 0.8]; progress = clamp((sY - newsTop + vh) / (vh + newsH), 0, 1); per item i: p = clamp((progress - stages[i]) / 0.15, 0, 1); opacity = p; translateY = (1-p) * 24px
newChoreographies.reducedMotion: disabled, items at default visible state

_risk-notes: Motion fatigue at 5 concurrent rAF loops + CSS view-block animations. Verify on low-end devices.
Layout shift propagation: any lazy-load that shifts heights will disrupt 5 timings simultaneously. Mitigations: explicit aspect-ratio + eager LCP + font-display swap with size-adjust.

divergence.amplifiedTrait: B3 inherits B's amplified-trait (scroll-driven choreography from samples' bizpro-hub) and intensifies its delivery across surface axes. Same captured trait, louder register.

surfaceForkAxes.delta: +1 weight on body H3s (700 vs B's 400)

divergence.brand_faithful_inversions: inherits-from-B (which inherits from A's 3: pure-surfaces / glassmorphism-nav / hex-not-OKLCH). B3 adds 0 new inversions.

guards.name: B-but-more
guards.rationale: B3 IS technically 'B but more' on motion-energy. The pattern is permitted here because B3 is documented as a surface fork (same captured-trait, intensified delivery), not a C+ slot (different captured-trait amplification). The C-cliff rule applies to C/D/E+ siblings under reimagined; B3 sits in a separate sub-variant lineage under B.

guards.name: motion-as-personality (overuse)
guards.rationale: B3's thesis IS motion saturation. The cap raised from B's ≤3 to ≤5 is the explicit acknowledgement. Risk notes document the motion-fatigue tradeoff.

guards.name: padding-as-personality
guards.rationale: B3's 48px is inside the multi-audience hard floor [40, 64]. Packed tier is a tuning, not personality.

extensions.iaPriorities: inherits-from-B (5 priorities, all movable under reimagined). B3 cannot opt out per Mode A.

do: Display headlines at clamp(48, 8vw, 108) — B3's hero claims more screen weight than A/B.
Below-fold sections each get a motion treatment (parallax on featured / 3-stage scrub on news).
Body H3s set at weight 700 — heavier register matches the loud surface.
dont: Adding a 6th rAF choreography — cap is ≤5 even in B3.
Below-fold sections without motion — B3's saturation is the thesis.
Below-fold animations slower than easeOut3 — B3's pacing is reactive, not editorial.

narrative.northStar: Adobe's full motion bench, on every primary surface. Same scroll-cinema thesis as B, intensified across type-scale, density, motion count, color temperature, and spacing rhythm.
narrative.overview: Surface fork of B. Inherits B → A's complete token stack (palette, type family, IA-priority audit, brand-faithful inversions). Variant-local overrides: perfect-fifth type scale (loudest canonical ratio), 700-weight body H3s, packed 48px section padding, 5 choreographies including new featured-products-parallax and news-scroll-scrub-3-stage. Cap override on B's ≤3-choreography rule, raised to ≤5 for B3 only.
keyCharacteristics: Same captured trait as B (scroll choreography), louder register.
Perfect-fifth type scale, packed density, compact spacing — loud surface.
Body H3s pick up to weight 700.
Below-fold saturation — every section gets a motion treatment.
Title-1 reaches 108px at extreme viewport (loudest display).
narrative.rules: inherits-from-B + cap-override-≤5-choreographies + below-fold-must-carry-motion + compact-rhythm-enforced + warm-leaning-placeholders + perfect-fifth-type-scale + body-H3-700
donts: Calm/cool placeholder gradients (warm-leaning is the temperature).
Title-1 below 80px max (perfect-fifth scale is the rule).
Section padding above 56px desktop (compact stays compact).
